title: Digital Services Taxes in Europe, 2026
summary: Currently, about half of all European OECD countries have either announced, proposed, or implemented a digital services tax. Because these taxes mainly impact US companies and are thus perceived as discriminatory, the US responded with retaliatory tariff threats.
ai_summary: Currently, about half of all European OECD countries have either announced, proposed, or implemented a digital services tax. Because these taxes mainly impact US companies and are thus perceived as discriminatory, the US responded with retaliatory tariff threats.

title: ITAT Chennai Allowed Section 80-IA Deduction as Consumer Tariff Held Correct Benchmark
summary: The Tribunal found that the transfer pricing adjustment was incorrectly computed using SEB sale rates. It allowed deduction based on consumer tariff rates. title: Section 271(1)(c) Penalty Cannot Be Imposed for Valuation Differences Without Proof of Inaccuracy: SC
summary: The Supreme Court held that mere differences in property valuation do not amount to furnishing inaccurate particulars. Penalty under Section 271(1)(c) requires proof of deliberate inaccuracy. The ruling clarifies that estimation disputes alone cannot justify penalty. The post Section 271(1)(c) Penalty Cannot Be Imposed for Valuation Differences Without Proof of Inaccuracy: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Supreme Court held that mere differences in property valuation do not amount to furnishing inaccurate particulars. title: Routine BPO Comparable Excluded for Functional Difference Reason; Different Year Comparable Considered on Quarterly Data Basis
summary: The Tribunal held that a company engaged in high-end KPO and business solutions could not be compared with a routine captive BPO service provider. It also ruled that a comparable cannot be rejected solely for following a different financial year if reliable quarterly data is available. The post Routine BPO Comparable Excluded for Functional Difference Reason; Different Year Comparable Considered on Quarterly Data Basis appeared first on TaxGuru .

title: Delay in Filing Form 10 Cannot Alone Defeat Section 11(2) Exemption Claim: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: The Tribunal ruled that exemption under Section 11(2) should not be denied solely due to delayed filing of Form-10. title: Calcutta HC Dismisses Tax Appeal Below ₹2 Crore Limit for Failure to Show CBDT Exception Applies
summary: Court found that the appellant failed to clearly identify which exceptional clause applied. Since the tax effect was below Rs.2 crores and no valid exception was demonstrated, the Court held that there was no reason to entertain the appeal. Accordingly, the appeal and the connected application were dismissed. The post Calcutta HC Dismisses Tax Appeal Below ₹2 Crore Limit for Failure to Show CBDT Exception Applies appeared first on TaxGuru .

title: Channel owners cannot be taken as comparables for distributors for transfer pricing purpose
summary: ITAT Delhi held that companies owning and operating channels cannot be compared with distribution company for the purpose of transfer pricing. Accordingly, directs TPO to exclude such comparables for benchmarking distribution segment and determination of ALP. The post Channel owners cannot be taken as comparables for distributors for transfer pricing purpose appeared first on TaxGuru .

title: Pass-Through Costs Excluded From Transfer Pricing PLI as No Value Addition Was Performed: ITAT Mumbai
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that payments made to third-party vendors on a cost-to-cost basis, without any value addition by the assessee, should be treated as pass-through costs and excluded from PLI computation. title: Cloud Service Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Due to No Transfer of IP Rights: SC
summary: The Supreme Court affirmed that payments for cloud computing services are not royalty where no intellectual property rights are transferred. It held that mere access to services does not amount to use of equipment or technology under tax law. The post Cloud Service Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Due to No Transfer of IP Rights: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .
